decisions.question: Task storage and state-transition atomicity
decisions.chosen: Per-task .lock file + authoritative state.json + append-only NDJSON transitions.log; flock → read → validate → atomic tmpfile-rename state.json → fsync parent → append log → unlock; worker auth via NIWA_TASK_ID env + state.json.worker.token (128-bit crypto/rand)
decisions.notes: Introduces NOT_TASK_EXECUTOR error code not in PRD R50; reconcile in Phase 3.

decisions.question: Daemon architecture
decisions.chosen: Central event loop + per-task supervisor goroutine (cmd.Wait() per worker); adopted orphans tracked centrally with 2s IsPIDAlive poll; watchdog-triggered kills consume retry slots; state.json authoritative, transitions.log observability

decisions.question: MCP topology and caller authentication
decisions.chosen: Per-session stdio MCP server + filesystem coordination + NIWA_TASK_ID env + state.json cross-check (worker_pid, worker_start_time, worker_role); rejects daemon-hosted server and separate token file
decisions.notes: Conflicts with D1's worker.token mechanism. Phase 3 reconcile: D3's approach (no token, use PID+start_time freshness) is the lower-complexity choice and eliminates the need for NOT_TASK_EXECUTOR.

decisions.question: Worker spawn contract
decisions.chosen: Literal-path NIWA_WORKER_SPAWN_COMMAND override + dual task-ID propagation (argv substitution + NIWA_TASK_ID env) + pass-through env with niwa-owned last-wins overrides

decisions.question: niwa-mesh skill installation and content
decisions.chosen: Flat uniform SKILL.md (no per-role variants, no references/ subdir); hash-based idempotency via existing InstanceState.ManagedFiles.ContentHash; mtime-stable re-apply via writeFileMode atomic pattern

decisions.question: Test harness contract and integration test scope
decisions.chosen: Literal-path NIWA_WORKER_SPAWN_COMMAND; MCP-client-driven scripted fake; niwa-owned daemon pause hooks for race-window AC; integer-seconds timing overrides with comma-list backoff; two residual @channels-e2e smoke scenarios (MCP-config loadability, bootstrap-prompt effectiveness)

decisions.question: Provisioning pipeline and migration
decisions.chosen: Hybrid blind-rewrite with opportunistic cleanup (~40-60 LOC helper); discard old-schema queued envelopes with one-shot warning; explicit cleanup of leftover .niwa/sessions/<uuid>/ dirs; runtime artifacts out of ManagedFiles

cross_validation_notes: D1 vs D3 conflict on worker auth resolved: adopted D3's NIWA_TASK_ID + state.json PPID/start-time check (mandatory on Linux, best-effort on macOS). D1's crypto token retired as redundant. D1's proposed NOT_TASK_EXECUTOR error code also retired — NOT_TASK_PARTY from PRD R50 is sufficient. All other cross-checks passed: D4 argv+env task-ID propagation consistent with D3's env-based identity; D6 scripted fake's MCP-client model consistent with D3's per-session stdio topology; D2 daemon writes to state.json consistent with D1's flock model; D5 skill artifact tracking consistent with D7's ManagedFiles discipline.
assumptions: 7 decisions — in the 6-7 scaling band. --auto proceeded with a single document.
Cross-validation will reconcile D1 vs D3 on worker authentication mechanism.
